sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"principles": ["Modern radiotherapy involves smaller fields and lower doses than historically used [TEST-C].", "CT-based AP-PA 3D-CRT has lower Dmean and D50% for kidneys, liver, and bowel compared to IMRT. IMRT is not necessary. Proton therapy can be considered [TEST-C].", "RT should start once orchiectomy wound has fully healed. Patients treated 5 days per week [TEST-C].", "Antiemetic prophylaxis encouraged at least 2 hours prior to each treatment [TEST-C].", "Discussion of semen analysis and sperm banking prior to treatment if clinically indicated [TEST-C].", "Non-contrast CT simulation with patient supine, arms at sides, in treatment position [TEST-C].", "All patients except those with bilateral orchiectomy should be treated with scrotal shield [TEST-C].", "Linear accelerators with >6 MV photons should be used when possible [TEST-C]."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Stage I Seminoma Adjuvant RT", "total_dose": "20 Gy (preferred), 25.5 Gy, 19.8 Gy, or 21.6 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "2.0 Gy, 1.5 Gy, 1.8 Gy, or 1.8 Gy respectively", "fractions": "10, 17, 11, or 12 respectively", "schedule": "Daily fractions over 2-3.5 weeks", "indications": "Adjuvant treatment for stage I seminoma after orchiectomy [TEST-C]."}, {"name": "Stage IIA Seminoma RT", "total_dose": "30 Gy (1-2 cm disease)", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8-2.0 Gy", "fractions": "Approximately 16-17 fractions", "schedule": "Two consecutive phases: modified dog-leg fields to 20-25.5 Gy then cone down", "indications": "Clinical stage IIA seminoma with non-bulky disease [TEST-C]."}, {"name": "Stage IIB Seminoma RT", "total_dose": "36 Gy (2-3 cm disease)", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8-2.0 Gy", "fractions": "Approximately 20 fractions", "schedule": "Two consecutive phases: modified dog-leg fields to 20-25.5 Gy then cone down", "indications": "Clinical stage IIB seminoma with non-bulky disease [TEST-C]."}], "approaches": [{"name": "Para-aortic Strip Fields RT (Stage I)", "dose_fractionation": "20 Gy in 10 fractions of 2.0 Gy each (preferred) or alternative schedules", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "None", "indication": "Stage I seminoma adjuvant treatment", "key_trial": "MRC TE18/EORTC 30942 trial comparing 20 Gy vs 30 Gy showing no difference in relapse rates [MS-10]", "toxicities": "Late toxicities including secondary malignancies, potential cardiovascular effects. Long-term follow-up studies indicate increased late toxicities with RT [MS-10]."}, {"name": "Modified Dog-Leg Fields + Cone Down (Stage IIA/B)", "dose_fractionation": "Initial phase: 20-25.5 Gy; Cone down to 30 Gy (IIA) or 36 Gy (IIB)", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "None (separate approach from chemotherapy)", "indication": "Clinical stage IIA/IIB seminoma", "key_trial": "Classen et al trial establishing standard field arrangements [MS-14]", "toxicities": "Acute: nausea, fatigue, diarrhea. Late: secondary malignancies, potential cardiovascular effects [MS-14]"}, {"name": "Carboplatin + Involved-Node RT (SAKK 01/10)", "dose_fractionation": "Single-dose carboplatin followed by involved-node RT", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Sequential, not concurrent", "indication": "Stage IIA/IIB seminoma (category 2B)", "key_trial": "SAKK 01/10: 3-year PFS 93.7% [SEM-3, MS-14]", "toxicities": "Grade 3-4: neutropenia 4%, thrombocytopenia 3%, vomiting 1%. No treatment-related deaths or late toxic effects reported [MS-14]"}]}

timeline.title: Stage I Seminoma Adjuvant RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 20 Gy (preferred), 25.5 Gy, 19.8 Gy, or 21.6 Gy. Fractions: 10, 17, 11, or 12 respectively. Daily fractions over 2-3.5 weeks.

timeline.title: Stage IIA Seminoma RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 30 Gy (1-2 cm disease). Dose per fraction: 1.8-2.0 Gy. Approximately 16-17 fractions. Two consecutive phases: modified dog-leg fields to 20-25.5 Gy then cone down.

timeline.title: Stage IIB Seminoma RT
timeline.body: Total dose: 36 Gy (2-3 cm disease). Dose per fraction: 1.8-2.0 Gy. Approximately 20 fractions. Two consecutive phases: modified dog-leg fields to 20-25.5 Gy then cone down.

timeline.title: Para-aortic Strip Fields RT (Stage I)
timeline.body: Dose/fractionation: 20 Gy in 10 fractions of 2.0 Gy each (preferred) or alternative schedules. Indication: Stage I seminoma adjuvant treatment.

timeline.title: Modified Dog-Leg Fields + Cone Down (Stage IIA/B)
timeline.body: Initial phase: 20-25.5 Gy; Cone down to 30 Gy (IIA) or 36 Gy (IIB). Indication: Clinical stage IIA/IIB seminoma.

timeline.title: Carboplatin + Involved-Node RT (SAKK 01/10)
timeline.body: Single-dose carboplatin followed by involved-node RT. Indication: Stage IIA/IIB seminoma (category 2B).
